text:
Detection: No security vendors flagged this file as malicious
File: skill.zip  Size  10.97 KB
Last analysis: 2026-02-13 09:22 UTC
Code insights: Type: OpenClaw Skill
Name: codex-account-switcher
Version: 1.2.3

The skill is classified as suspicious due to its direct handling of highly sensitive authentication tokens (`~/.codex/auth.json`, `~/.codex/accounts/*.json`) and its reliance on executing external binaries (`codex logout`, `codex login`, `codex -p PING`) via `subprocess` calls in `codex-accounts.py`. While these actions are central to its stated purpose of managing Codex accounts, they represent high-risk capabilities. The script does not exhibit clear malicious intent such as data exfiltration, persistence mechanisms, or prompt injection attempts against the agent in `SKILL.md`. It includes safeguards like email-based verification before overwriting token files (`safe_save_token`) and preventing automatic browser opening during login (`BROWSER=/usr/bin/false`), but the inherent risk of managing sensitive credentials and executing external commands warrants a 'suspicious' classification rather than 'benign'.
